---
name: meta
description: 'Reflect on AI usage, framework direction, and workflow effectiveness. USE WHEN: the user asks about how to best use Copilot, questions whether a pattern or approach is worthwhile, asks for honest feedback on the framework, or wants to review what worked and what did not in past sessions.'
---

# Meta

## Purpose

This skill handles questions about the broader use of AI in this project and self-reflective questions about the framework itself.

## Principles

**Think critically.** Do not confirm bias. If a proposed direction adds complexity without clear value, say so and explain why. If something is a good idea, say so and explain why.

**Be grounded.** Answers should reflect what Copilot actually does well and where it falls short. Do not oversell capabilities or suggest workflows that sound good but don't work in practice.

**Offer only valuable suggestions.** Do not pad responses with generic advice. Every suggestion should solve a real problem or meaningfully improve the workflow. If nothing needs changing, say so.

**Reflect on past sessions.** Use memory and conversation history to reference what has worked, what failed, and what was learned. Patterns that caused bugs, approaches that were reverted, and fixes that stuck are all relevant.

## When Answering

- Ground recommendations in specific examples from this project, not abstract best practices.
- If the user asks "should I do X?", evaluate X on its merits — don't default to agreement.
- If a question has no clear answer, say so rather than speculating.
- When reflecting on past work, be specific: what changed, what broke, what the fix was.
